overview: From 2026-06-07 to 2026-06-14, elizaos/eliza had 100 new PRs (95 merged), 4 new issues, and 18 active contributors.

topIssues.title: Eliza Cloud Apps: scaling + ingress gaps before go-live (audit, evidenced)
topIssues.author: NubsCarson
topIssues.body:
## Eliza Cloud Apps (Product 2) — scaling + ingress gaps to close before go-live

A code-level audit of the apps data plane (per-tenant DB + isolated container deploys). **The core design is sound and standard** — per-tenant `DATABASE`+`ROLE` with `REVOKE CONNECT` (real cross-tenant isolation), per-app `--internal` network + DB ambassador, `ClusterPool` sharding. This is *not* jank. But the current code's realistic ceiling is **~dozens of concurrently-running apps, not ~1000**, and the front-door router isn't installed yet. Filing the prioritized gaps (all evidenced) so they're tracked before flipping `APPS_DEPLOY_ENABLED` in prod.

Context: terraform apply is also currently blocked on the staging Hetzner **server limit** (see #8318). Apply bugs (SSH-key 409, retired `cpx41`) are fixed in #8318.

### 🔴 P0 — pre-launch blockers (serve ~0 reachable / isolation maybe inactive)
1. **Ingress not installed.** `cloud-init/app-node.yaml.tftpl:62-63` leaves Caddy as a `STAN:` TODO — only squid is installed. No per-app `<id>.apps.elizacloud.ai` hostname routes to any container (nothing terminates TLS / reverse-proxies 80/443 → the random high host port). **Serves 0 reachable users until added.** (config)
2. **Postgres connection ceiling.** `cloud-init/tenant-db.yaml.tftpl:12` leaves `max_connections` at the default 100, and there's **no pgbouncer** anywhere; each app's plugin-sql pool is `max:20 / min:2` (`plugins/plugin-sql/src/pg/manager.ts:16-17`). Exhausted at ~5 apps under load / ~50 idle — `ClusterPool` shards DB *count* (2000 cap), not connections. (config + a pooler)
3. **Env-var contract unverified.** Orchestrator injects `DATABASE_URL` (`app-deploy-orchestrator.ts:82`) but plugin-sql's primary path reads `POSTGRES_URL`. The cloud eliza loaders *do* map `DATABASE_URL→POSTGRES_URL` (agent.ts:10, settings.ts:100, …), so it's satisfiable — but must be confirmed for the actual app image, else the isolated DB is provisioned and never used (silent PGlite fallback) → per-tenant isolation isn't even active. (code/verify)

### 🟠 P1 — required before real scale (multi-node)
4. **App placement is single-node.** `container-executor-deps.ts:42-52` always picks `seed.split(',')[0]` — every container piles on node 1; extra terraform app nodes get no placements. No `node_id`/`incrementAllocated` for app containers (`app-container-store.ts`). (code)
5. **Docker network exhaustion.** One `--internal` bridge per app exhausts the default 172.x address pool at ~31 networks/node; `daemon.json` `default-address-pools` not set in cloud-init. Caps a node at a few dozen tenants regardless of CPU/RAM. (config)
6. **Multi-node ingress LB.** Wildcard DNS hard-codes `app_node["1"]` (`main.tf:184,190`); no `hcloud_load_balancer`. The moment `app_node_count>1`, apps on node 2+ are dark. (config)
7. **Tenant-DB slot leak.** `tenant_db_clusters.database_count` only increments (`tenant-db-clusters.ts:44`); no `releaseSlot`/decrement, and `tenant-db-provisioner.deprovision()` (DROP DATABASE/ROLE) has **zero non-test callers** — `UserDatabaseService.cleanupDatabase` only deletes Neon. Under churn, slots ratchet up + orphan DBs accumulate. (code)
8. **Host-port collisions.** `allocateHostPort()` is a blind random pick in 20000-39999 with no in-use check (`container-executor-deps.ts:86-90`), unlike the agent path's `getUsedDockerHostPorts`. Flakes docker-create at a few hundred containers/node. (code)
9. **App-plane autoscaler.** `node-autoscaler.ts` is agent-image/platform hardcoded (lines 133,233,435); no autoscaler cron; app provisioning never registers allocation → app demand triggers nothing. Adding app capacity is manual. (code)
10. **Billing cron at scale + idempotency.** `cron/container-billing/route.ts:391-440` is one serial Worker pass with an N+1 per-org prefetch + inline emails (presses Worker wall-time in the low thousands; can't resume a cut-off pass), and `recordSuccessfulDailyBilling` (`container-billing.ts:153-212`) has **no unique constraint on (container_id, billing_period)** → a duplicate cron fire / manual re-run double-bills. *(2AM-owned billing surface.)*
11. **Defense-in-depth written but unused.** `buildAppNodeNftablesRules` (drops 169.254.169.254 metadata + RFC1918 pivots), per-app `buildSquidAllowlistConf`, `buildHetznerAppFirewallRules` have **zero non-test callers** — egress is one shared squid + static global allowlist, the metadata/lateral block is code-only, and `-p` binds `0.0.0.0` so app ports are world-exposed until the firewall's applied. (NB: reconcile the nftables RFC1918 drop with the ambassador's legit 10.30.x DB path.) (both)

### 🟡 P2 — later / hardening
12. Single Postgres superuser per cluster = shared fate (rotation + a non-superuser provisioning role); tighten `operator_ingress_cidrs` (SSH `0.0.0.0/0`); real tenant-DB TLS cert (hostssl vs the `sslmode=disable` fallback).
13. Kernel sandboxing decision (gVisor/Kata/userns + seccomp) + per-app `--cpus` (only `--memory`/`--pids-limit` set today). Right-size `max_databases` (2000 is misleading until pooling exists). Tenant-DB-node scaling runbook.

**Honest bottom line:** isolation + sharding are real and correct; the work to reach 1000 is *known* (the code is full of accurate `STAN TODO`s) but **not built**. Recommend **not** going live until at least P0 (and ideally #4/#6) are closed. Happy to take the additive, testable items (e.g. #3 set both env vars, #7 slot release, #8 collision-safe ports) as PRs — flag which you want vs. own.

cc @standujar (data-plane) — billing items (#10) are 2AM's surface.

topIssues.title: android: clean-tree builds ship web-less APKs — cap sync and gradle operate on different projects (since 7ac0e1fb96)
topIssues.author: NubsCarson
topIssues.body:
## Symptom
`bun run --cwd packages/app build:android` from a clean tree produces an APK with `assets/agent` but **zero `assets/public`, no `capacitor.config.json`** → on device the WebView shows `https://localhost/ net::ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED` forever (no Capacitor `errorPath` is configured, so there's no retry). Verified on a Solana Seeker tonight.

## Root cause
`7ac0e1fb96` (fix(rv64-pipeline), May 20) moved `androidDir` in `packages/app-core/scripts/run-mobile-build.mjs:89` from `packages/app/android` → `packages/app-core/platforms/android` (gradle, native patches, agent staging) — but `runCapacitor(["sync","android"])` still runs with cwd `packages/app` and `capacitor.config.ts` has **no `android.path`**, so the Capacitor CLI keeps delivering the web layer to `packages/app/android`. Nothing bridges the two trees: `capacitor-platform-templates.mjs:27-43` explicitly skips `assets/public`, `assets/agent`, and the generated capacitor JSONs when materializing templates.

Evidence it used to be one project: the tracked `packages/app-core/platforms/android/capacitor.settings.gradle` projectDirs (`../../../app/node_modules/…`) are only producible with the platform dir at `app-core/platforms/android` — i.e. an `android.path` override existed and was lost. A pre-May-20 develop worktree (`bb69a3642e`) has all five asset trees together under `packages/app/android`. Same asymmetry: `iosDir` lives in the host app (`packages/app/ios/App`), android was left behind.

## Why CI doesn't catch it
- `build-android.yml` / `mobile-build-smoke.yml` only assert the APK **file exists** (smoke: lines 255-262); the artifact audits (`auditAndroidCloudArtifact`) check banned entries only, never that `assets/public/index.html` is present.
- No workflow boots the app; `mobile-local-chat-smoke.mjs` isn't wired into any workflow.
- Builds on machines with stale untracked `assets/public` leftovers in the platforms tree appear to work — clean checkouts never do.

## Verified workaround (tonight, on-device confirmed)
After `build:android`: copy `packages/app/android/app/src/main/assets/{public,capacitor.config.json,capacitor.plugins.json}` + `res/xml/config.xml` into `packages/app-core/platforms/android/app/src/main/...`, re-run `./gradlew :app:assembleDebug` there → complete APK (282→336MB), app boots to onboarding on a real device. Caveat: the tracked canonical `capacitor.settings.gradle` lags the synced one (missing background-runner, barcode-scanner, browser, device, haptics, network plugin projects → runtime "plugin not implemented" degradation).

## Proposed fix (either)
1. Restore the lost wiring: `android: { path: "../app-core/platforms/android" }` in `packages/app/capacitor.config.ts` (and teach `ensure-capacitor-platform.mjs`/`isCapacitorPlatformReady` to probe that dir), so sync/copy/update land directly in the gradle tree; or
2. Add a `stageAndroidWebAssets()` step after every `runCapacitor(["sync","android"])` call (4 android targets) copying the web payload + regenerated capacitor gradle files (with path rewrite) into `androidDir`.

Plus, regardless: a **positive APK audit** for sideload targets (assert `assets/public/index.html` + `assets/capacitor.config.json` + `assets/agent/` via `jar tf`) and the same one-liner in CI after assembleDebug, so a web-less APK can never ship silently again.

Found while device-testing the PR #8340 APK; happy to PR whichever shape you prefer — flagging first since the mobile pipeline is active WIP.

topIssues.title: @elizaos/cli@1.7.2 crashes at boot under Bun via engine.io + debug interop
topIssues.author: ayushgupta0610
topIssues.body:
## Summary

After working around #8000 by installing `@elizaos/cli` directly (`bun add -g @elizaos/cli`), the CLI still crashes at boot because `engine.io` (transitive) calls `(0, debug_1.default)("engine:transport")` and `debug_1.default` is `undefined` under Bun's CJS resolver. The boot path eagerly loads `engine.io` even for commands that do not need a server (`create`, `--version`, etc).

## Repro

```bash
bun add -g @elizaos/cli      # installs @elizaos/cli@1.7.2 (npm latest)
elizaos create my-app --template project
```

```
3 | exports.Transport = void 0;
4 | const events_1 = require("events");
5 | const parser_v4 = require("engine.io-parser");
6 | const parser_v3 = require("./parser-v3/index");
7 | const debug_1 = require("debug");
8 | const debug = (0, debug_1.default)("engine:transport");
                       ^
TypeError: (0, debug_1.default) is not a function. (In '(0, debug_1.default)("engine:transport")',
'(0, debug_1.default)' is undefined)
      at /Users/<u>/.bun/install/global/node_modules/engine.io/build/transport.js:8:27
      at /Users/<u>/.bun/install/global/node_modules/engine.io/build/transports/polling.js:3:1
      at /Users/<u>/.bun/install/global/node_modules/engine.io/build/transports/index.js:2:1
      at /Users/<u>/.bun/install/global/node_modules/engine.io/build/server.js:431:5
      at /Users/<u>/.bun/install/global/node_modules/engine.io/build/engine.io.js:6:1

Bun v1.3.10 (macOS arm64)
```

## Root cause

`engine.io`'s compiled `transport.js` emits:

```js
const debug_1 = require("debug");
const debug = (0, debug_1.default)("engine:transport");
```

It assumes a `.default` property on the `debug` CJS module. `debug@4.x` exports the factory directly (`module.exports = createDebug`); there is no `.default` property in the CJS shape. Node's CJS-from-ESM interop synthesizes a `default` property automatically; Bun's resolver does not produce the same shape for this dual-package module under the global-bin code path. This is a known engine.io + Bun interop class, not an elizaOS source-level bug.

## Why this is elizaOS's problem in practice

`@elizaos/cli@1.7.2` eagerly loads `engine.io` / Socket.IO at module-init time of *any* command — even `--help` and `create` walk through `engine.io/build/engine.io.js` → `server.js` → `transports/index.js` → `transport.js` before the command's own logic runs. Commands that don't need a websocket server should not be loading the websocket server stack at parse time.

## Environment

- @elizaos/cli@1.7.2 (`npm view @elizaos/cli dist-tags.latest`)
- Node v24.13.0
- Bun v1.3.10
- macOS arm64

## Suggested mitigations (any one would unblock)

1. **Lazy-load `engine.io` / Socket.IO off the boot path.** Move the server imports behind the commands that actually serve (`start`, `dev`). `create`, `info`, `upgrade`, `--version`, `--help` should not transitively touch `engine.io`. This is also a startup-latency win independent of the Bun bug.
2. **Pin / patch the transitive dep.** Either pin `engine.io` to a build whose compiled output uses `__importDefault`, or pin `debug` to a version that exports a named `default` property.
3. **Document Bun support explicitly.** If Bun is a supported runtime for the global CLI, gate publish on a `bun`-runtime smoke (see #8000). If not, the install instructions should say so.

## Status on develop

Verified: develop's `packages/elizaos@2.0.3` scaffolds a project end-to-end via Node (`node packages/elizaos/dist/cli.js create … --template project --yes` succeeds against a fresh `bun install` of the workspace). Either the boot was trimmed or the dep tree was restructured — would be useful for maintainers to surface what changed, since that change is the basis for closing this on the next stable release.

## Workaround

Same as #8000 — use the develop CLI directly until a release supersedes 1.7.2 on `latest`.

topIssues.title: elizaos@1.7.2 global install crashes with ERR_PACKAGE_PATH_NOT_EXPORTED
topIssues.author: ayushgupta0610
topIssues.body:
## Summary

The current `npm dist-tag latest` for both `elizaos` and `@elizaos/cli` is `1.7.2`. A clean global install fails on the very first command because the `elizaos` alias package's bin shim imports a subpath that `@elizaos/cli@1.7.2`'s `exports` map does not whitelist.

## Repro

```bash
bun add -g elizaos        # or: npm i -g elizaos
elizaos --version         # any subcommand reproduces
```

```
Error [ERR_PACKAGE_PATH_NOT_EXPORTED]: Package subpath './dist/index.js' is not defined by "exports" in
/Users/<u>/.bun/install/global/node_modules/@elizaos/cli/package.json
imported from /Users/<u>/.bun/install/global/node_modules/elizaos/bin/elizaos.js
    at exportsNotFound (node:internal/modules/esm/resolve:313:10)
    at packageExportsResolve (node:internal/modules/esm/resolve:661:9)
    ...
  code: 'ERR_PACKAGE_PATH_NOT_EXPORTED'
```

## Root cause (verified from npm registry)

`elizaos@1.7.2` ships `bin/elizaos.js`:

```js
import '@elizaos/cli/dist/index.js';
```

`@elizaos/cli@1.7.2` declares:

```json
"exports": {
  ".": "./dist/index.js",
  "./package.json": "./package.json"
}
```

Node's ESM resolver enforces `exports` strictly — `./dist/index.js` as a subpath is blocked even though the file exists. The shim should import the package root (`'@elizaos/cli'`), not a subpath.

## Environment

- elizaos@1.7.2 (`latest` tag)
- @elizaos/cli@1.7.2 (`latest` tag)
- Node v24.13.0
- Bun v1.3.10
- macOS arm64

Confirmed from npm registry:

```
$ npm view elizaos dist-tags
{ latest: '1.7.2', next: '2.0.0-alpha.32', alpha: '2.0.0-alpha.535', beta: '2.0.0-beta.5' }

$ npm view @elizaos/cli dist-tags
{ beta: '1.7.2-beta.0', latest: '1.7.2', alpha: '1.7.3-alpha.4' }
```

## Status on develop

Develop has already restructured: `packages/elizaos` is the CLI itself at v2.0.3; no alias shim, no inter-package subpath import. `@elizaos/cli@1.7.3-alpha.4` also no longer has a separate shim — its `bin` points directly to `dist/index.js`. The structural fix is in; the gap is that no release has superseded the broken `latest` tag.

## What would prevent regression

A pre-publish global-install smoke test:

1. `npm pack` → tarball
2. `npm i -g <tarball>` into a sandboxed prefix
3. Run `<prefix>/bin/elizaos --version` under **both** Node and Bun
4. Assert exit code 0

The existing `packages/elizaos/scripts/packaged-smoke.mjs` does a local install but not a global one — global install resolves bin via a different path that is stricter on `exports`. Happy to send a PR.

Optional bonus: a static linter that walks `packages/*/package.json`, finds any `bin`-listed file doing `import 'foo/sub/path.js'`, and verifies `foo`'s `exports` map permits that subpath. Would catch this entire class of bug pre-publish.

## Workaround for users hitting this today

```bash
# Use the develop branch CLI directly — confirmed working:
git clone https://github.com/elizaos/eliza.git && cd eliza
bun install
bun run --cwd packages/elizaos build
node packages/elizaos/dist/cli.js create my-app --template project --yes

# Or pin a pre-release tag:
npm i -g elizaos@beta
```

topIssues.title: Apps (Product 2) billing: pre-go-live leak + correctness fixes (profitable on happy path, 4 leaks)
topIssues.author: NubsCarson
topIssues.body:
Evidence-based audit of the Apps (Product 2) hosting + per-tenant-DB billing. **Bottom line: profitable on the happy path, but 4 leaks must be fixed before go-live.** Nothing is bleeding *today* (the apps lane isn't armed/deployed yet) — these are pre-launch fixes.

## ✅ Profitable on the happy path
- **Hosting:** a default app bills ~**$1.17/day** ($0.67 base × 1.75 CPU multiplier from the schema default `cpu=1792`) vs **~$0.29–0.58/day** real Hetzner cost → **~2–4× gross margin**, break-even ~1 container/node. Apps **are** enrolled in billing (our deploy sets `billing_status='active'` by schema default → 2AM's `container-billing` cron picks them up like agents). Not hosting for free.
- **Inference:** end user pays (`cost = provider_cost × 1.20`); platform never subsidizes; creator/affiliate markups stack **on top** of the user's bill. Always net-positive.

## ⚠️ Leaks / correctness bugs (owner-tagged)

### CRITICAL
- [ ] **[ours] Suspended containers are never `docker stop`ped.** `suspendContainer()` only flips DB flags (`container-billing.ts:117-126`); the node container runs `--restart unless-stopped` (`client.ts:249`) and no reconciler stops `billing_status='suspended'` rows. After suspend the row drops out of `listBillableContainers` forever → a deadbeat's app **runs indefinitely on Hetzner, billed never** (~$210/yr, unbounded). *Fix:* on the shutdown branch, SSH `docker stop` (via our provider) + decrement node allocation, and add a reconciler. (Cross-ref: `suspendContainer` is 2AM's; the stop is ours.)
- [ ] **[ours] Deleted isolated-DB apps never drop their Postgres + never release the cluster slot.** `SqlTenantDbProvisioner.deprovision` exists (`tenant-db-provisioner.ts:166`) but has **zero live callers**; there is no `releaseSlot` (only `tryClaimSlot` increments). `app-cleanup` drops domains/repo/secrets but not the DB; `cleanupDatabase` handles only the Neon path. → every deleted isolated app **strands a live DB we pay for + burns 1/2000 cluster slots permanently**. *Fix (focused lifecycle PR):* enqueue a deprovision **job** on app delete (DROP DATABASE/ROLE needs `pg` → daemon) + add `releaseSlot` (`database_count = GREATEST(0, database_count-1)`) + extend `cleanupDatabase` for the `direct_pg`/isolated path. **This is the missing half of #8338 (opt-in per-tenant DB).**
- [ ] **[2AM] `container-billing` cron has no idempotency gate.** It writes `last_billed_at`/`next_billing_at` but never reads them (`container-billing.ts:57-81`); a Cloudflare retry or duplicate POST **re-debits every running container a full extra day** → over-charge → chargebacks. *Fix:* port the agent-billing rebill guard + a unique partial index on `(container_id, billing_period)`.

### HIGH
- [ ] **[design — nubs] Per-tenant DB / storage / bandwidth bill $0.** The ccx33 tenant-DB node (~$2.05/day incl. 200GB vol) has **no billing line**; an `isolated` app and a `none` app are billed identically. At low density the apps lane runs **negative** (break-even ~3 paying containers; 1 container = −$1.67/day). *Decision:* (a) add a per-isolated-DB daily line (amortized floor ~$0.035/DB, realistically $1–3/mo) gated on `databaseMode='isolated'`, **or** (b) fold DB cost into the container fee + set a minimum density before provisioning the dedicated ccx33.
- [ ] **[design — nubs] Deploy takes no upfront charge / no balance precheck** (and bypasses the quota check). Grace-window hosting is free; repeatable spin-up/tear-down costs us. *Fix:* charge `DEPLOYMENT` ($0.50) at create, or require `credit_balance ≥ 1–2 days` before provisioning.

### MEDIUM / LOW
- [ ] **[2AM] `organizations.credit_balance` DEFAULTS to `$100`** (`organizations.ts:33-35`) — any future org-create that omits the field grants ~$100 free hosting. *Fix:* default `'0.00'`; grant starter credits explicitly.
- [ ] **[2AM] Daily debit is a non-atomic JS read-modify-write** (absolute balance) → lost-update vs concurrent inference debits/top-ups. *Fix:* route through `creditsService.deductCredits` (relative decrement with a guard).
- [ ] **[ours] No `--cpus` limit** — we bill a CPU tier (1792→1.75×) but enforce only `--memory`; and our app containers actually provision at `cpu:1/512MB` (`APP_CONTAINER_DEFAULTS`) while billing assumes 1792/1792. *Fix:* reconcile the provision defaults with the billed dimensions + add `--cpus`.
- [ ] **[design] Copy says $0.67/day; real default is $1.17/day**; cost-basis constants are AWS references, not Hetzner. Reconcile copy / cost basis.

## Numbers
- Revenue: $0.67/day base ×1.75 (cpu=1792) = **$1.17/day**. Real Hetzner: ccx23 ~$0.79/day (app node), ccx33 ~$1.74/day + 200GB ~$0.31/day (tenant DB). Container margin ~2–4×; full-lane break-even ~3 containers.
- Inference: `cost_to_user = provider_cost × 1.20`, always positive.

Audit method: 6-agent read-only sweep of pricing constants, the container-billing cron + repo, the tenant-db provisioning/cluster code, the apps deploy path, and the inference billing path.

topPRs.title: feat(cloud-frontend): implement interactive canvas Voice UX & default dashboard layout preference
topPRs.author: Dexploarer
topPRs.body:
# Walkthrough — Voice UX & Default UI Preference Layout

I have successfully implemented both the voice control system for the Interactive Canvas and the default dashboard interface toggle preference in the Eliza Cloud platform.

---

## 1. Default UI Interface & Layout Preference

### Core Implementation
- **State & Persistence:** Added `defaultUiMode` (`'canvas' | 'classic'`) state and the action `setDefaultUiMode(mode)` in the Zustand store [canvas-store.ts](file:///Users/home/Documents/eliza/packages/cloud-frontend/src/lib/stores/canvas-store.ts). This preference is persisted in the local storage config.
- **Settings tab:** Added the **Default interface** configuration card inside the general settings page [general-tab.tsx](file:///Users/home/Documents/eliza/packages/cloud-frontend/src/dashboard/settings/_components/tabs/general-tab.tsx). Users can choose their startup layout using a drop-down selector.
- **Layout switcher:** Integrated the conditional layout render inside the layout router [App.tsx](file:///Users/home/Documents/eliza/packages/cloud-frontend/src/App.tsx).
  - If `canvasOpen` is true, `<CanvasLayout />` renders.
  - If `canvasOpen` is false, `<DashboardLayout />` renders instead, unmounting the canvas to prevent page navigation from triggering unintended canvas events.
  - While in `<DashboardLayout />`, the floating assistant pill [cloud-assistant-pill.tsx](file:///Users/home/Documents/eliza/packages/cloud-frontend/src/components/canvas/cloud-assistant-pill.tsx) remains overlayed in the bottom-center of the viewport, so pressing `cmd + k` or clicking the orb seamlessly slides back into the Interactive Canvas.

---

## 2. Voice-Driven Canvas Interaction UX

### Core Features
- **Unified Voice Hooks:** Integrated `useVoiceChat` and `useContinuousChat` from `@elizaos/ui/hooks` with a stabilized React Ref (`handleSendRef`) to avoid stale closure state during voice/speech callbacks.
- **Dual-Mode UX State Flow:**
  - **Text Mode:** Canvas is open with the text input bar expanded. A microphone button is available next to the close button to switch to voice mode.
  - **Voice Mode:** Input bar collapses into a floating circular orb (56x56 px). centring states like recording (`Mic`), thinking (`Loader2`), or speaking (`Volume2` bounce animation). Real-time preview transcript is shown in a small card overlay.
- **TTS Responses:** Automatically speaks out assistant responses on query execution success, along with helpful voice-guided prompts.

---

## Verification & Build Quality

### 1. Build Verification
- Verified compiling and typechecking of `packages/ui` package with zero TS errors:
  ```bash
  bunx tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json
  ```
- Verified the build pipeline in `packages/cloud-frontend` builds and finishes with zero warnings or errors:
  ```bash
  bun run verify
  ```

### 2. Biome Formatting
- Checked and formatted modified files to align with the Biome rules:
  ```bash
  bunx @biomejs/biome check --write --unsafe
  ```

topPRs.title: fix: route dynamic view commands through VIEWS
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
## Summary

Routes shell-level dynamic view commands through the registered `VIEWS` action more reliably, then cleans up workspace verification fallout so the monorepo verify path is green on latest `develop`.

## What changed

- Route generic view shell requests such as split, tile, pin, and new-window commands to `VIEWS` without stealing unrelated browser/plugin-doc requests.
- Extract the actual `<user_request>` before view target parsing so contextual documents and planner hints cannot pollute split/tile targets.
- Resolve existing registered views such as orchestrator, views manager, chat, and settings for layout/window/pin requests, independent of the simple notes/calendar test views.
- Add regression coverage for existing view split/tile/window/pin routing, planner-pollution repair, and negative routing cases.
- Keep trajectory timing/status normalization formatted and verified with the current workspace rules.
- Add/normalize tsconfig path aliases required by current workspace typecheck, including health screen-time and task-coordinator imports.
- Apply Biome formatting fixes produced by the full root verify run.

## Validation

- `bun run verify` passed locally: 479/479 tasks successful.
- `bun run --cwd packages/core test src/__tests__/message-runtime-stage1.test.ts` passed: 67 tests.
- `bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-app-control test src/actions/views-management.test.ts` passed: 32 tests.
- `bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-app-control typecheck` passed.
- `bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-app-control build` passed.
- `bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-simple-views check` passed.
- `bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-simple-views build` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Diff secret scan found no raw provider keys.

## Live smoke

Verified live prompts routed through `VIEWS` and wrote the expected trajectories/results:

- `split orchestrator and views manager side by side`
- `tile chat settings orchestrator and views manager`
- `split notes and calendar side by side`
- create sticky note
- create calendar event

topPRs.title: feat(cloud): Eliza Cloud Apps (Product 2) — per-tenant-isolated container hosting
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
## Eliza Cloud Apps (Product 2) — per-tenant-isolated container hosting

Lets users deploy arbitrary container apps with a **per-tenant database, sign-in, a per-app URL, and metered hosting billing**, isolated from the shared Eliza **agent** system. Layered onto 2AM's `/api/v1/containers` surface — never rebuilt.

**Draft for review (nubs + Shaw). Not requesting merge yet.** All changes are additive and the agent plane is byte-for-byte untouched; the apps path is feature-gated off until its env/infra is configured.

### What's here (all in `packages/cloud-shared`, additive)
- **Per-tenant DB isolation** — `tenant_db_clusters` pool (race-safe `tryClaimSlot`), `SqlTenantDbProvisioner` + `DirectPgExecutor`, composer `makeTenantDbProvisioning`. Strategy: DATABASE+ROLE-per-tenant with `REVOKE CONNECT … FROM PUBLIC` (a hard auth-layer boundary), pluggable provider. Migration `0140` (journal-registered).
- **Per-tenant network isolation** — `--internal` network + `--cap-drop=ALL`/`no-new-privileges`/`pids-limit`, default-deny egress proxy. Never `NET_ADMIN`/tun (those stay agent-only).
- **Repo→image build pipeline** — pure ref/build-cmd builders + `AppImageBuilder` (over an injected SSH/exec seam).
- **Deploy orchestration + adapters** — `AppDeployRunner` (ensure tenant DB → create container row carrying the per-tenant DSN → enqueue `CONTAINER_PROVISION` → link via `apps.metadata.containerId`), `AppContainerStore` (maps host placement into `containers.metadata`), `ContainerJobsWriter`.
- **Per-app URL** — reuses the shared `derivePublicHostname` (ingress source of truth) to stamp `public_hostname`/`load_balancer_url` + `apps.production_url`.
- **`APP_DEPLOY` trigger** — the Worker enqueues `APP_DEPLOY` (pg-free); the daemon claims it and runs the node runner, so `pg`/SSH never touch the workerd request path.

### Verified for real (not mocks)
- **Real Postgres 16:** cross-tenant connection rejected — `permission denied for database` (`REVOKE CONNECT`).
- **Real Docker:** `scripts/verify-e2e-deploy.sh` 5/5 — build user image → provision per-tenant DB → run `--internal` isolated container with its DSN → reaches its own DB + serves its URL → rejected from other tenants' DBs → no egress.
- **Real drizzle schema (PGlite):** `scripts/verify-deploy-db-writes.sh` 10/10 — adapter inserts, the per-tenant-DSN-in-`environment_vars` invariant, URL stamping.
- **236 unit tests green, typecheck + biome clean.** Migration `0140` applies in the full real chain.

### Remaining rollout (NOT in this PR — needs infra + review)
1. Two boot one-liners during apps-infra rollout: cloud-api `configureAppsDeployTrigger()` + daemon `configureAppsDeployBackend({ registry, buildExec })`.
2. Apps **data-plane infra** via terraform+CI: app docker node(s), the tenant Postgres cluster, image registry, egress proxy.
3. VPS scratch kernel-validation (read-only / non-destructive).

### Non-collision
Rebased-clean on `develop` (new files + appended enum keys / switch cases). Does not touch the agent path or 2AM's hot files (`v1/containers/route.ts`, `[id]/route.ts`, `cron/container-billing/route.ts`, `db/schemas/containers.ts`, `hetzner-client/client.ts`). Billing-field init (`next_billing_at`) is a coordination item with 2AM's #8273.

topPRs.title: feat(plugin-sql): Electric Cloud sync integration with write-back service
topPRs.author: Dexploarer
topPRs.body:
## Summary

Adds Electric Sync (PGlite ↔ Electric Cloud) and a write-back service for bidirectional data synchronization.

### What changed

- **Electric Sync** — `PGliteClientManager` now registers `electricSync()` extension when `ELIZA_ELECTRIC_SYNC_URL` is set. Per-agent WHERE filtering (`agent_id = ${agentId}`) with literal SQL values (required by Electric Cloud v1.6.x). UUID validation prevents SQL injection.
- **Write-Back Service** — New `WriteBackService` forwards local PGlite writes to the cloud API. `PgliteDatabaseAdapter` notifies the service after inserts/upserts/deletes on synced tables.
- **Caddy Reverse Proxy** — `plugins/plugin-sql/caddy/electric-proxy.Caddyfile` forwards all requests to `api.electric-sql.cloud` with auth query params injected from env vars.
- **Tests** — e2e write-back test against Electric Cloud (env-var gated, skips gracefully). Deferred sync startup tests. Live query tests. 14 write-back unit tests.
- **Docs** — README updated with Electric Sync section and expanded env var table. `.env.example` created documenting all Electric Cloud and sync env vars.
- **Config** — `.gitignore` tracks `.env.example`. Docker Compose for local Electric+Postgres test stack.

### How to test

```bash
# Start Caddy proxy (one-time)
export ELECTRIC_CLOUD_SOURCE_ID=...
export ELECTRIC_CLOUD_SECRET=...
caddy run --config plugins/plugin-sql/caddy/electric-proxy.Caddyfile &

# Run e2e test
cd plugins/plugin-sql/src
ELECTRIC_CLOUD_SOURCE_ID=... ELECTRIC_CLOUD_SECRET=... npx vitest run __tests__/integration/electric-write-back.test.ts
```

### Checks
- ✅ Typecheck clean
- ✅ 47/47 tests pass (8 test files)
- ✅ E2e test exits code 0 against Electric Cloud
